title: Assistant Project Cost Analyst
description:
Objective

To support project teams by monitoring costs at the pre-commitment and post-commitment stages, ensuring expenditures are aligned with approved budgets, identifying cost variances early, and supporting effective cost control and reporting.

Key Responsibilities

Cost Monitoring & Budget Control

  • Review Purchase Requisitions (PRs) and Purchase Orders (POs) against approved project budgets and cost codes prior to commitment.
  • Check that proposed costs are within approved budget limits and highlight potential overruns or abnormal pricing.
  • Monitor committed costs versus budget, including tracking PRs, POs, and variations.
  • Identify cost trends, repeated cost increases, or unusual pricing compared to historical data or previous hull/projects.
  • Support price comparison by reviewing quotations, technical/commercial evaluations, and cost breakdowns submitted by Procurement team.
  • Assist in verifying cost reasonableness for change orders, repeat orders, or scope variations.
  • Work with procurement, project and commercial teams to obtain justifications for cost increases or budget deviations.
  • Support reconciliation of committed cost versus actual cost where required.
  • Prepare cost summaries, variance reports, and simple dashboards and potential cost impacts.
  • Ensure cost-related documentation (approved budgets, justifications, approvals) is properly filed and traceable.

Required Skills & Competencies

  • Understand project cost control, budgeting, and procurement processes.
  • Strong analytical skills with attention to detail in cost comparison and variance analysis.
  • Ability to work with numbers, spreadsheets, and cost data accurately.
  • Good communication skills to coordinate with project, procurement, and finance teams.
  • Familiarity with ERP systems (e.g. Workday, SAP, Navision) and Excel is essential.
  • Organised, systematic, and able to manage multiple cost items and projects concurrently.

postedCompany.description:
Penguin is a Singaporean homegrown, publicly listed designer, builder, owner and operator of high-speed aluminium craft, ranging from standardised stock vessels to complex bespoke designs. Through a group of wholly owned, integrated subsidiaries, we own and operate a fleet of crewboats, ferries and workboats, and we build a variety of high-speed craft at our shipyards in Singapore and Batam, Indonesia.


Our business is backed by a strong balance sheet and led by an experienced hands-on management team. We are committed to maintaining a high standard of public accountability and social responsibility. We stand for integrity, professionalism, and mutual respect.


We value our employees and their talents. We evaluate job candidates based on their skills and experience, as well as their potential to fit into our corporate culture.


Our headquarters in Tuas is part of Penguin’s integrated shipyard and office complex located at 21 Tuas Road. It is easily accessible via AYE and MRT (Gul Circle station, Green Line).
postedCompany.name: PENGUIN INTERNATIONAL LIMITED
